background: A sample of 3 units is drawn without replacement from a batch of 15 units, of which two are bad. Let X denote the number of bad units in the sample.

task.query: Determine the probability function of X.
task.answer:
(i) $P(X=0)=\frac{13}{15} \cdot \frac{12}{14} \cdot \frac{11}{13}=\frac{22}{35}$

Let g and b denote good and bad units, respectively. We obtain

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(X=1) & =P\{g, g, b\}+P\{g, b, g\}+P\{b, g, g\} \\
& =\frac{13}{15} \cdot \frac{12}{14} \cdot \frac{2}{13}+\frac{13}{15} \cdot \frac{2}{14} \cdot \frac{12}{13}+\frac{2}{15} \cdot \frac{13}{14} \cdot \frac{12}{13} \\
& =\frac{12}{35}
\end{aligned}
$$

Similarly,

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(X=2) & =P\{g, b, b\}+P\{b, g, b\}+P\{b, b, g\} \\
& =\frac{13}{15} \cdot \frac{2}{14} \cdot \frac{1}{13}+\frac{2}{15} \cdot \frac{13}{14} \cdot \frac{1}{13}+\frac{2}{15} \cdot \frac{1}{14} \cdot \frac{13}{13} \\
& =\frac{1}{35}
\end{aligned}
$$

Thus, the probability function of $X$ is

$$
P(X=x)=\left\{\begin{array}{l}
\frac{22}{35} \quad x=0 \\
\frac{12}{35} \quad x=1 \\
\frac{1}{35} \quad x=2
\end{array}\right.
$$

task.query: Determine the distribution function of X.
task.answer:
(ii) The probability function of $X$ is

$$
F(x)= \begin{cases}0 & x<0 \\
\frac{22}{35} & 0 \leq x<1 \\
\frac{34}{35} & 1 \leq x<2 \\
1 & x \geq 2\end{cases}
$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
